text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet village surrounded by dense pine forests, lived a shepherd named Leo. He possessed the keenest eye a shepherd needed and a heart as gentle as the gentlest murmur of a river.

Every morning Leo would wake up before dawn, stretch, and feed his flock. His days were filled with shepherding, but Leo also had moments of solitude that he cherished.

One such morning, Leo gazed out over the valley as the sun peeked over the mountains. He felt a deep tranquility and decided it was perfect time for a walk. Taking a sturdy staff in hand, he embarked on his daily pilgrimage through the whispering woods.

Midway, he came across a clearing and stopped. Nestled under an old oak were three golden-brown bears. They hadn't moved all day, seeming content just looking at each other. Leo looked around— there were no humans around for miles.
